title: Feature-Market Fit — Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services (NAICS 518210)

sections.title: Step 09 — Technology-Level Feature-Market Fit
sections.content:
Technology-scope features (01_product_decomposition) scored against JTBD_data_processing_hosting error statements (ES-01..ES-26). Safety-of-IT-load dominates the top opportunity stack — scoring weights reflect GPU thermal-runaway context.

### 09a — Per-Market Kano + ODI Fit Scoring

| Feature | Kano | Time | Cost | Safety | Reliab. | Skill | Stress | Pain | Confid. | Delight | Overall |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Transit-Time Flow Measurement** | must_be | 6 | 6 | 8 | 8 | 4 | 6 | 5 | 8 | 3 | **6.00** |
| **Integrated Temperature Sensing** | attractive | 7 | 8 | 6 | 7 | 6 | 5 | 7 | 7 | 6 | **6.56** |
| **Zero Pressure Drop Flow Path** | one_dimensional | 3 | 6 | 3 | 4 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 4 | 3 | **3.56** |
| **No Moving Parts** | one_dimensional | 4 | 8 | 7 | 9 | 5 | 7 | 8 | 8 | 5 | **6.78** |
| **Bidirectional Flow Detection** | attractive | 4 | 5 | 7 | 5 | 4 | 5 | 4 | 6 | 5 | **5.00** |
| **Digital Anomaly Detection** | attractive | 8 | 7 | 9 | 7 | 7 | 8 | 6 | 9 | 7 | **7.56** |
| **Real-Time Compensation Algorithms** | must_be | 3 | 5 | 5 | 7 | 5 | 4 | 4 | 6 | 3 | **4.67** |

### 09a — Rationales

**Transit-Time Flow Measurement** (must_be, 6.00): Commodity baseline — every flow alternative in JTBD-2.4 delivers flow quantification. Must_be because a CDU without flow telemetry cannot safely gate GPU load. Primary drivers: Reliability (8) and Confidence (8) — OCP 1.5 L/min/kW threshold (ES-08, imp=9) enforcement; Safety (8) — loss-of-flow detection <2s (ES-11, opp=16). [SRC: JTBD_dph ES-08, ES-11; OCP guidance]

**Integrated Temperature Sensing** (attractive, 6.56): Directly addresses ES-12 (sub-second thermal duty kW = flow × ΔT from a single body, imp=8, opp=12, prod_related). Eliminates separate temperature probe and synchronization error between two sensors feeding the DCIM PUE calculation. Primary drivers: Cost (8) — removes second sensor BOM and wiring; Pain (7) — single commissioning touchpoint; Delight (6) — "kW out of one can" is genuinely surprising to CDU integrators. [SRC: JTBD_dph ES-12, ES-18]

**Zero Pressure Drop Flow Path** (one_dimensional, 3.56): CDU secondary-loop pressure drop is dominated by the brazed-plate HX, cold-plate manifolds, and hoses — sensor contribution <50 mbar is marginal at 30 L/min (ES-13, imp=7, opp=8). Linear benefit to pump energy / PUE, but small magnitude. Primary driver: Cost (6) — pump energy savings. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition, JTBD_dph ES-13]

**No Moving Parts** (one_dimensional, 6.78): High-value differentiator vs turbine/impeller incumbents (15% share) in 24×7 GPU fleet. Addresses ES-09 (±2% over 40,000 h, imp=9, opp=13). Bearing wear in turbine sensors silently degrades the flow reading that gates the OCP safety threshold. Primary drivers: Reliability (9) — eliminates mechanical failure modes in the loop that protects GPU thermal envelope; Pain (8) and Cost (8) — removes periodic replacement of in-loop sensors across hundreds of rack CDUs; Confidence (8) — stable calibration reduces false-throttle events (ES-16). [SRC: JTBD_dph ES-09, ES-16]

**Bidirectional Flow Detection** (attractive, 5.00): Addresses ES-15 (reverse-flow detection within 1s, imp=7, opp=10, prod_related) — detects bypass-valve misoperation, mis-piped commissioning, or pump-failure siphon. Turbine and thermal-dispersion alternatives cannot resolve direction. Primary drivers: Safety (7) — detects anti-flow condition before GPU load energizes; Confidence (6) — unambiguous direction signal in the OCP safety interlock chain. [SRC: JTBD_dph ES-15]

**Digital Anomaly Detection** (attractive, 7.56): **Highest-scoring feature.** Directly serves the top of the opportunity stack: empty-pipe pre-IT-power-on (ES-06, opp=15, prod_related), air entrainment after glycol top-up (ES-10, opp=12, prod_related), incipient leak signatures (ES-20, opp=14, prod_related). Traditional mechanical meters provide zero diagnostics. Primary drivers: Safety (9) — the empty-pipe and sub-threshold-leak signals are the gate and early-warning layer that prevent GPU thermal runaway; Confidence (9) — controller knows when a zero-flow reading is real vs diagnostic; Stress (8) — reduces operator anxiety during workload ramps. [SRC: JTBD_dph ES-06, ES-10, ES-20]

**Real-Time Compensation Algorithms** (must_be, 4.67): Must_be because CDU secondary loops run water, PG25, or EG25 blends configured at commissioning (ES-03, prod_related). Without speed-of-sound compensation, a glycol-concentration change silently biases kW duty and PUE telemetry. Primary driver: Reliability (7) — reduces fluid-property-induced error from ~1.5% to ~0.2%. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition; JTBD_dph ES-03]

### 09b — Validation Summary

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| Kano cross-market consistency | N/A — single market |
| safety >= 7 AND confidence <= 3 | PASS — Digital Anomaly Detection: safety=9, confidence=9 (consistent) |
| skill >= 7 AND stress <= 3 | PASS — no violations |
| overall_fit arithmetic | PASS — all recomputed |
| No feature scores 10 on all dimensions | PASS |
| Feature names comply with 1.pdf rules | PASS — spec-visible noun phrases, no brands, no outcome claims |
| Safety-critical coherence | PASS — Transit-Time, No Moving Parts, and Digital Anomaly all score safety >= 7, aligned with market's top 5 needs being safety-driven |

---

sections.title: Step 10 — Product-Related Needs (Burleson Product Jobs)
sections.content:
Needs that exist BECAUSE of ultrasonic transit-time technology. Each traces to a constraint in 01_product_decomposition. Litmus test: would the need disappear with electromagnetic or Coriolis measurement? If yes, retain.

### 10a — Constraint-Derived Needs

| ID | Source Constraint | Statement | Imp. | Sat. | Litmus |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| PRN-01 | Media Limitation | Minimize the likelihood of measurement drift from acoustic-impedance mismatch when facility-specific glycol inhibitor packages (corrosion, antimicrobial) alter the coolant's speed of sound beyond the commissioned compensation profile | 7 | 4 | PASS — electromagnetic (Faraday) unaffected by sound speed |
| PRN-02 | Maximum Medium Temperature | Minimize the residual headroom concern when future ASHRAE W4/W5 warm-water liquid cooling drives CDU secondary-loop supply to the 90 °C polymer housing limit, capping the sensor's applicability in evolving high-efficiency thermal envelopes | 5 | 5 | PASS — metal-housed magnetic/Coriolis meters tolerate 130 °C+ |
| PRN-03 | Minimum Flow Rate Detection | Minimize the unmeasured-flow window at the DN20 4.2 L/min SNR threshold during CDU idle / low-load states (single-GPU workloads, redundant-pump slew), below which the OCP 1.5 L/min/kW interlock cannot discriminate "low flow" from "no flow" | 8 | 4 | PASS — magnetic meters resolve sub-0.1 m/s, thermal switches binary |
| PRN-04 | Air Bubble Tolerance | Minimize the probability of sub-threshold air entrainment (1–2 % vol) following glycol top-up or venting biasing flow telemetry fed to the OCP safety interlock, without triggering the >2 % loss-of-signal alarm | 8 | 3 | PASS — electromagnetic meters insensitive to air content |
| PRN-05 | Pipe Full Condition | Minimize the time the CDU controller spends in empty-pipe fault state during secondary-loop drain-and-fill maintenance cycles, during which the sensor provides no measurement and the prove-flow gate cannot be satisfied | 7 | 4 | PASS — mechanical impellers still rotate in partial fill |
| PRN-06 | Minimum Straight Pipe Run | Minimize the installation-induced flow-profile bias from insufficient upstream straight-pipe run (<5× DN) inside space-constrained rack CDU manifolds where single-chord acoustic path sampling cannot correct for swirl | 7 | 5 | PASS — multi-path ultrasonic or magnetic meters less path-profile sensitive |
| PRN-07 | Temperature-Dependent Accuracy | Minimize the transient kW duty miscalculation when a GPU workload spike drives secondary-supply temperature transients >5 °C/min that outpace the NTC thermistor's compensation response, during which flow × ΔT telemetry deviates from true thermal load | 7 | 5 | PASS — magnetic measurement is temperature-independent by principle |
| PRN-08 | Maximum Operating Pressure | Minimize the deployment gap at hyperscaler facility-loop pressures exceeding 10 bar (high-head centralized-pump architectures, water-hammer on rapid valve closure) where the polymer housing rating forecloses direct-facility-loop installation and forces placement downstream of the brazed-plate HX | 5 | 4 | PASS — metal-bodied magnetic/Coriolis withstand 16–40 bar |

### 10a — Deduplication Check

| Step 10 Need | JTBD P1 Need | Overlap? | Resolution |
|---|---|---|---|
| PRN-01 (glycol inhibitor acoustic drift) | ES-03 (fluid acoustic commissioning) | Different scope | ES-03 is commissioning-time configuration (product_related); PRN-01 is ongoing in-service drift from inhibitor chemistry |
| PRN-02 (90 °C ASHRAE W4/W5 headroom) | — | Unique | Forward-looking constraint gap |
| PRN-03 (SNR threshold vs OCP) | ES-08 (OCP 1.5 L/min/kW) | Different level | ES-08 is the market safety rule; PRN-03 is the ultrasonic-specific detection-floor consequence |
| PRN-04 (sub-threshold air bias) | ES-10 (air entrainment signal loss, prod_related) | Complementary | ES-10 covers signal-loss alarm; PRN-04 covers silent bias below that threshold |
| PRN-05 (empty-pipe maintenance blackout) | ES-06 (empty-pipe pre-IT power-on, prod_related) | Different phase | ES-06 is pre-energization gate; PRN-05 is in-service maintenance measurement gap |
| PRN-06 (single-chord path bias in rack CDU) | ES-04 (installation orientation bias) | Different mechanism | ES-04 is generic orientation; PRN-06 is ultrasonic-specific path sampling in compact manifolds |
| PRN-07 (speed-of-sound transient) | ES-21 (acoustic-coupling drift, prod_related) | Different timescale | ES-21 is 5-year drift; PRN-07 is sub-minute transient lag |
| PRN-08 (10 bar facility-loop pressure) | — | Unique | Facility-side placement constraint |

### 10b — Validation Summary

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| Litmus test (all needs technology-specific) | PASS — all 8 disappear with electromagnetic, Coriolis, or vortex technology |
| Source constraint traceability | PASS — each traces to a named constraint in 01_product_decomposition |
| Affected market plausibility | PASS — all 8 manifest in CDU secondary-loop context |
| Completeness (every constraint covered) | PASS — all 8 constraints each generate one need |
| No importance < 3 AND satisfaction > 7 | PASS |

---

sections.title: Step 11 — Vendor Feature-Market Fit (Marquardt vs Incumbent)
sections.content:
Vendor-scope features scored against NAICS 518210 CDU liquid-cooling market. Best incumbent: **Endress+Hauser Promag H (electromagnetic)** — magnetic transit-time is the leading alternative in DLC/CDU secondary-loop flow metering (~35 % share, rising with AI GPU rack deployment). [SRC: JTBD_dph-2.4; Mass Flow Online; IoThrifty]

### 11a — Vendor vs Incumbent Comparison

| Feature | Kano | Vendor | Incumbent | Inc. Technology | Advantage |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Modular Pipe Diameter Variants** | one_dimensional | 5 | 8 | Electromagnetic (E+H Promag H) | **inferior** |
| **Customizable Communication Interface** | must_be | 3 | 9 | Electromagnetic (E+H Promag H) | **inferior** |
| **Customizable Mechanical Interface** | attractive | 7 | 6 | Electromagnetic (E+H Promag H) | **superior** |
| **Low-Energy Battery Mode** | indifferent | 6 | 2 | Electromagnetic (E+H Promag H) | **parity** |
| **Pre-Calibrated Delivery** | must_be | 7 | 8 | Electromagnetic (E+H Promag H) | **parity** |
| **KTW/WRAS Drinking Water Approval** | indifferent | 7 | 4 | Electromagnetic (E+H Promag H) | **parity** |

### 11a — Rationales

**Modular Pipe Diameter Variants** (one_dimensional, inferior): Marquardt DN12/14/20/25 shares RAST 2.5 electronics, but only DN20 is serial-production. CDU OEMs (Vertiv, nVent, Motivair) need DN15–DN25 for secondary-loop and DN6–DN10 for cold-plate taps — Marquardt covers the secondary-loop band only, single production variant. E+H Promag H covers DN2–DN150 in full production with the same transmitter. Addresses ES-02 (single platform DN15–DN25, imp=7, prod_related). [SRC: 01_product_decomposition]

**Customizable Communication Interface** (must_be, inferior): **Largest capability gap.** CDU BMS stacks standardize on Modbus RTU/TCP, BACnet/IP, MQTT, and increasingly OPC-UA for DCIM integration (ES-05, imp=7). Marquardt ships UART/LIN/frequency — protocols native to automotive/appliance OEM, with zero BMS overlap. E+H Promag H provides HART, Modbus, PROFIBUS/PROFINET, EtherNet/IP out of the box. Must_be in this market — CDU integrators will not design a custom gateway for a sensor. [SRC: JTBD_dph ES-05]

**Customizable Mechanical Interface** (attractive, superior): Threaded and plug-in connections fit the CDU OEM assembly line — rack CDUs use compact manifolds with quick-connect fittings for field-replaceable modules. E+H Promag H uses flanged or threaded industrial fittings sized for process-plant installation, not rack-CDU serviceability. Marquardt's plug-in option directly serves ES-22 (replace sensor without draining loop or taking CDU offline, imp=8, opp=13). [SRC: 01_product_decomposition; JTBD_dph ES-22]

**Low-Energy Battery Mode** (indifferent, parity): CDUs are mains-powered — battery operation is not a requirement for this market. Marquardt's <5 μA mode is a capability without a buyer here; E+H Promag H is loop-powered with HART and does not support battery operation. Both score on a dimension that does not matter — hence parity on an indifferent Kano class. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition]

**Pre-Calibrated Delivery** (must_be, parity): Marquardt ships 100 %-automated final-test calibration to customer-specified medium. E+H Promag H delivers factory-calibrated with 3.1 material certificates and optional ISO 17025 traceability — the CDU-integrator standard. Both satisfy the commissioning-ready baseline (ES-04, imp=8). No MID-equivalent is required in data-center cooling (unlike district energy), so Marquardt's calibration package is competitive. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition]

**KTW/WRAS Drinking Water Approval** (indifferent, parity): CDU secondary loops use treated, inhibited coolant (DI water, PG25, EG25), not potable water. The certification is not a purchase criterion in this market. Both scores reflect that neither vendor's potable-water position drives adoption — parity on an indifferent dimension. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition]

### 11b — Objectivity Review

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| Vendor bias (>70 % superior) | PASS — 1 superior / 3 parity / 2 inferior (17 % superior). Marquardt is an HVAC/appliance OEM sensor entering a data-center market dominated by E+H / Siemens / Krohne. |
| Anti-vendor bias (>70 % inferior) | PASS — 33 % inferior. Justified: Marquardt's protocol suite and variant coverage lag the purpose-built industrial line. |
| Advantage classification arithmetic | PASS — all gaps verified |
| Kano must_be scores >= 5 | FAIL on Customizable Communication Interface — vendor=3 on must_be. Flagged as primary integration barrier, not scoring error. |
| Rationale specificity | PASS — all reference specific vendor specs vs incumbent capabilities |

### Market Entry Assessment Summary

| Dimension | Verdict | Key Issue |
|---|---|---|
| Technology fit | **Strong** | Ultrasonic transit-time is the second-leading CDU flow technology (30 % and rising); no-wear + integrated temp + digital diagnostics directly address the top-5 safety needs |
| Product fit | **Strong** | DN20, 90 °C, 10 bar envelope matches rack-CDU secondary-loop operating point (W3/W4 ASHRAE class, 3–6 bar typical); constraint set is compatible with the present GPU-cooling deployment reality |
| Vendor fit | **Weak** | Missing Modbus/BACnet/MQTT protocols — single largest adoption barrier; serial production limited to DN20; no data-center reference deployments |
| Competitive position | **Component OEM to CDU makers** | Strongest go-to-market: supply sensor module to Vertiv / nVent / Motivair / CoolIT with a protocol-bridge firmware variant (UART → Modbus) on the CDU controller side. Avoid direct sale to hyperscalers until an OT-ready communication variant exists. |

---

sections.title: QA Checklist
sections.content:
| # | Check | Result |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | All 7 technology-scope features assessed | PASS |
| 2 | Kano classification with rationale per feature | PASS |
| 3 | 9-dimension ODI scores with recomputed overall_fit | PASS |
| 4 | Product-related needs trace to specific constraints | PASS — all 8 trace to named constraints |
| 5 | Litmus test applied to all product-related needs | PASS — all disappear with different technology |
| 6 | No duplication with JTBD P1 needs | PASS — deduplication table provided |
| 7 | Vendor features assessed against best incumbent | PASS — E+H Promag H (electromagnetic, 35 % share) |
| 8 | Objectivity review on vendor scoring | PASS — 17 % superior, no bias detected |
| 9 | Source tags on all factual claims | PASS |
| 10 | Web search NOT used | PASS — all derived from upstream files |

---

sections.title: Sources
sections.content:
- 01_product_decomposition.md — Features, constraints, specifications, UNSPSC classification
- JTBD_data_processing_hosting.md — CFJ, job map, alternatives, error statements (ES-01..ES-26)
- VN_data_processing_hosting.md — Process VN, CDU parent L5, flow-sensor anchor
- Marquardt Customer Presentation: "US Flow Sensor" (03.12.2025, CONFIDENTIAL)
- OCP liquid-cooling guidance: 1.5 L/min/kW [via JTBD_dph]
- Mass Flow Online, IoThrifty — magnetic vs ultrasonic share in DLC/CDU

rows.Result: N/A — single market
PASS — Digital Anomaly Detection: safety=9, confidence=9 (consistent)
PASS — no violations
PASS — all recomputed

rows.Check: No feature scores 10 on all dimensions
rows.Result: PASS
PASS — spec-visible noun phrases, no brands, no outcome claims
PASS — Transit-Time, No Moving Parts, and Digital Anomaly all score safety >= 7, aligned with market's top 5 needs being safety-driven

rows.Statement: Minimize the likelihood of measurement drift from acoustic-impedance mismatch when facility-specific glycol inhibitor packages (corrosion, antimicrobial) alter the coolant's speed of sound beyond the commissioned compensation profile
rows.Litmus: PASS — electromagnetic (Faraday) unaffected by sound speed

rows.Statement: Minimize the residual headroom concern when future ASHRAE W4/W5 warm-water liquid cooling drives CDU secondary-loop supply to the 90 °C polymer housing limit, capping the sensor's applicability in evolving high-efficiency thermal envelopes

Minimize the unmeasured-flow window at the DN20 4.2 L/min SNR threshold during CDU idle / low-load states (single-GPU workloads, redundant-pump slew), below which the OCP 1.5 L/min/kW interlock cannot discriminate "low flow" from "no flow"
rows.Litmus: PASS — magnetic meters resolve sub-0.1 m/s, thermal switches binary

rows.Statement: Minimize the probability of sub-threshold air entrainment (1–2 % vol) following glycol top-up or venting biasing flow telemetry fed to the OCP safety interlock, without triggering the >2 % loss-of-signal alarm
rows.Litmus: PASS — electromagnetic meters insensitive to air content

rows.Statement: Minimize the time the CDU controller spends in empty-pipe fault state during secondary-loop drain-and-fill maintenance cycles, during which the sensor provides no measurement and the prove-flow gate cannot be satisfied
rows.Litmus: PASS — mechanical impellers still rotate in partial fill

rows.Statement: Minimize the installation-induced flow-profile bias from insufficient upstream straight-pipe run (<5× DN) inside space-constrained rack CDU manifolds where single-chord acoustic path sampling cannot correct for swirl
rows.Litmus: PASS — multi-path ultrasonic or magnetic meters less path-profile sensitive

rows.Statement: Minimize the transient kW duty miscalculation when a GPU workload spike drives secondary-supply temperature transients >5 °C/min that outpace the NTC thermistor's compensation response, during which flow × ΔT telemetry deviates from true thermal load
rows.Litmus: PASS — magnetic measurement is temperature-independent by principle

rows.Statement: Minimize the deployment gap at hyperscaler facility-loop pressures exceeding 10 bar (high-head centralized-pump architectures, water-hammer on rapid valve closure) where the polymer housing rating forecloses direct-facility-loop installation and forces placement downstream of the brazed-plate HX

rows.Resolution: ES-03 is commissioning-time configuration (product_related); PRN-01 is ongoing in-service drift from inhibitor chemistry
ES-08 is the market safety rule; PRN-03 is the ultrasonic-specific detection-floor consequence
ES-10 covers signal-loss alarm; PRN-04 covers silent bias below that threshold
ES-06 is pre-energization gate; PRN-05 is in-service maintenance measurement gap

rows.Step 10 Need: PRN-06 (single-chord path bias in rack CDU)
rows.Resolution: ES-04 is generic orientation; PRN-06 is ultrasonic-specific path sampling in compact manifolds
ES-21 is 5-year drift; PRN-07 is sub-minute transient lag

rows.Result: PASS — all 8 disappear with electromagnetic, Coriolis, or vortex technology
PASS — each traces to a named constraint in 01_product_decomposition
PASS — all 8 manifest in CDU secondary-loop context
PASS — all 8 constraints each generate one need
PASS
PASS — 1 superior / 3 parity / 2 inferior (17 % superior). Marquardt is an HVAC/appliance OEM sensor entering a data-center market dominated by E+H / Siemens / Krohne.
PASS — 33 % inferior. Justified: Marquardt's protocol suite and variant coverage lag the purpose-built industrial line.
PASS — all gaps verified
FAIL on Customizable Communication Interface — vendor=3 on must_be. Flagged as primary integration barrier, not scoring error.
PASS — all reference specific vendor specs vs incumbent capabilities

rows.Key Issue: Ultrasonic transit-time is the second-leading CDU flow technology (30 % and rising); no-wear + integrated temp + digital diagnostics directly address the top-5 safety needs
DN20, 90 °C, 10 bar envelope matches rack-CDU secondary-loop operating point (W3/W4 ASHRAE class, 3–6 bar typical); constraint set is compatible with the present GPU-cooling deployment reality
Missing Modbus/BACnet/MQTT protocols — single largest adoption barrier; serial production limited to DN20; no data-center reference deployments

Strongest go-to-market: supply sensor module to Vertiv / nVent / Motivair / CoolIT with a protocol-bridge firmware variant (UART → Modbus) on the CDU controller side. Avoid direct sale to hyperscalers until an OT-ready communication variant exists.

rows.Result: PASS

rows.Check: Kano classification with rationale per feature
rows.Result: PASS
PASS

rows.Check: Product-related needs trace to specific constraints
rows.Result: PASS — all 8 trace to named constraints

rows.Check: Litmus test applied to all product-related needs
rows.Result: PASS — all disappear with different technology

rows.Check: No duplication with JTBD P1 needs
rows.Result: PASS — deduplication table provided

rows.Check: Vendor features assessed against best incumbent
rows.Result: PASS — E+H Promag H (electromagnetic, 35 % share)
PASS — 17 % superior, no bias detected

rows.Check: Source tags on all factual claims
rows.Result: PASS

entities.naics_title: Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services
entities.market_name: Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services

entries.market_name: Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services
entries.rationale: Commodity baseline — every flow alternative quantifies flow. Must_be because CDU cannot safely gate GPU load without it. Primary drivers: Reliability (8), Confidence (8) — OCP 1.5 L/min/kW threshold; Safety (8) — loss-of-flow <2s (ES-11, opp=16).

entries.market_name: Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services
entries.rationale: Addresses ES-12 (flow × ΔT kW duty from single body, opp=12, prod_related). Eliminates separate temp probe and sync error in PUE calculation. Primary drivers: Cost (8) — removes second sensor BOM; Pain (7) — single commissioning touchpoint; Delight (6) — 'kW from one can'.

entries.market_name: Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services
entries.rationale: CDU loop ΔP dominated by brazed-plate HX, cold-plate manifold, hoses. Sensor's <50 mbar contribution is marginal (ES-13, opp=8). Linear pump-energy/PUE benefit but small magnitude. Primary driver: Cost (6).

entries.market_name: Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services
entries.rationale: High-value differentiator vs turbine/impeller (15% share) in 24x7 GPU fleet. Addresses ES-09 (±2% over 40,000h, opp=13). Bearing wear silently degrades the OCP gating signal. Primary drivers: Reliability (9), Cost (8), Pain (8) — eliminates fleet-wide in-loop sensor replacement; Confidence (8) — reduces false-throttle events.

entries.market_name: Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services
entries.rationale: Addresses ES-15 (reverse-flow <1s, opp=10, prod_related) — detects bypass-valve misoperation, mis-piped commissioning, pump-failure siphon. Turbine and thermal switch cannot resolve direction. Primary drivers: Safety (7), Confidence (6).

entries.market_name: Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services
entries.rationale: Highest-scoring feature. Serves top opportunity stack: empty-pipe pre-IT power-on (ES-06, opp=15), air entrainment post-glycol top-up (ES-10, opp=12), incipient leak (ES-20, opp=14). Mechanical meters have zero diagnostics. Primary drivers: Safety (9), Confidence (9) — the prevent-thermal-runaway layer.

entries.market_name: Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services
entries.rationale: Must_be — CDU loops run water/PG25/EG25 configured at commissioning (ES-03, prod_related). Without speed-of-sound compensation, glycol-concentration change silently biases kW duty and PUE. Primary driver: Reliability (7) — reduces error from ~1.5% to ~0.2%.
